method: single-binary env-toggle removal-oracle: identical gzippy run under two native Mach-O wrappers; env flips ONE code path; output byte-identical (sha==gzip -dc on both arms); freq-pinned warmed multi-cohort paired A/B; verdict REPRODUCIBLE only if effect clears the gz-vs-gz A/A cross-cohort floor in every cohort with consistent sign.

verdicts.pmull: CONFIRMED wall win (silesia-T1 +1.22%, logsbig-T1 +1.89%; both clear A/A floor every cohort). NOT microbench-only.
verdicts.tablebuild: CONFIRMED wall win (silesia-T1 +0.89%, logsbig-T1 +4.71%; both clear floor every cohort).
verdicts.crossover: CORPUS-DEPENDENT, NOT a clean win. T1 cells INERT (env never consulted). At T2: silesia WIN +1.58% (default-OFF parallel beats forced-serial) but logsbig REGRESSION -8.54% (default-OFF parallel LOSES to forced-serial). The ratio proxy cannot separate them (silesia ratio~3.1, logsbig ratio~3.7) yet their optimal T2 routing is opposite.

pmull.claim: aarch64 default CRC = PMULL carry-less fold (vs crc32x 3-way fold3)
pmull.oracle: GZIPPY_CRC_PMULL=0 forces fold3 (change removed); default = PMULL (present)

tablebuild.claim: aarch64 default litlen table build = SINGLE-symbol (vs x86 TRIPLE-pack)
tablebuild.oracle: GZIPPY_LITLEN_MULTISYM=triple forces x86 default (removed); default = SINGLE (present)

crossover.claim: aarch64 default parallel-crossover selector = OFF (margin 0.0; vs x86/Zen2 ON margin 1.0)
crossover.oracle: GZIPPY_PARALLEL_CROSSOVER_MARGIN=1.0 forces x86/Zen2 selector ON (removed=selector-on); default = OFF (present)
crossover.gate0_non_inert: T1 INERT (effective_parallel_threads early-returns at num_threads<=1, env never read) -> measured at T2 where CONFIRMED non-inert (default->2 parallel, removed->1 serial)
